title: lathyrus pannonicus var. paucijugus
intro: Lathyrus pannonicus var. paucijugus, also known as the Hungarian vetch or Hungarian pea, is a rare and lesser-known variety of the Lathyrus pannonicus plant, a species of flowering plant in the pea family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include diuretic, expectorant, and anti-inflammatory effects. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as relieving respiratory issues, reducing fever, and aiding in the treatment of gastrointestinal disorders. Lathyrus pannonicus var. paucijugus can be used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
